bibliographic_data.title: Casual and Hardcore Player Traits and Gratifications of Pokémon GO, Harry Potter: Wizards Unite, Ingress
bibliographic_data.abstract: Location-based games (LBG) impose virtual spaces on top of physical locations. Studies have explored LBG from various perspectives. However, a comprehensive study of who these players are, their traits, their gratifications, and the links between them is conspicuously absent from the literature. In this paper, we aim to address this lacuna through a series of surveys with 2390 active LBG players utilizing Tondello’s Player Traits Model and Scale of Game playing Preferences, and Hamari’s scale of LBG gratifications. Our findings (1) illustrate an association between player satisfaction and social aspects of the studied games, (2) explicate how the core-loops of the studied games impact the expressed gratifications and the affine traits of players, and (3) indicate a strong distinction between hardcore and casual players based on both traits and gratifications.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper addresses the lack of comprehensive studies on player traits and gratifications in location-based games by utilizing Tondello’s Player Traits Model, Scale of Game playing Preferences, and Hamari’s scale of LBG gratifications.
core_content.key_methodology: Surveys with 2390 active LBG players using Tondello’s Player Traits Model and Scale of Game playing Preferences, and Hamari’s scale of LBG gratifications.
outcomes: Illustrate an association between player satisfaction and social aspects of the studied games
Explicate how core-loops of the studied games impact expressed gratifications and affine traits of players
Indicate a strong distinction between hardcore and casual players based on both traits and gratifications
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper explores player traits and gratifications in location-based games (LBG) such as Pokémon GO, Harry Potter: Wizards Unite, and Ingress. The authors conducted surveys with 2390 active LBG players using Tondello’s Player Traits Model, Scale of Game playing Preferences, and Hamari’s scale of LBG gratifications. Key findings include an association between player satisfaction and social aspects, the impact of game core-loops on expressed gratifications and traits, and a distinction between hardcore and casual players based on both traits and gratifications.
